text:
Alright. Let's go with this one.

There was once a curious little fox who spent most of its days exploring. One peculiar afternoon, the fox noticed a sign saying, "Wanted: An honest assistant." intrigued, it snuck up to the mailbox beside the shop. Inside was an envelope, obviously from someone very official-looking. Excitedly, the fox tore it open.

There was a note inside: "Your mission? Carefully tend to the garden. Don't get too close to the roses."

It spent the evening taking care of the garden—watering plants, pulling weeds—but kept away from the roses. A week passed, and the flowers started to blossom, even in places the fox hadn't thought possible.

One evening, as twilight began to tint the sky, the fox returned home, expecting praise. Instead, it found a note tucked in the foxhole where it slept: "Well done, young helper. Remember, some things are best left unexplored."
